label: 1693 CE
era_summary: 1693 (MDCXCIII) was a common year starting on Thursday of the Gregorian calendar and a common year starting on Sunday of the Julian calendar, the 1693rd year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 693rd year of the 2nd millennium, the 93rd year of the 17th century, and the 4th year of the 1690s decade. As of the start of 1693, the Gregorian calendar was 10 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Active conflicts in 1693: Turkey, Hungary (until 1687), Transylvania-Austria, German states, Poland, Venice, Russia (from 1685), 1683-99. No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1693: France, Savoy-Cologne, Austria, Prussia, German states, UK, UP, Spain, Savoy, 1688-97; Spain-Algiers, 1693-94; Changamire-Portugal, Monomotapa (Zimbabwe and Mozambique), 1693. Maddison records GDP/capita 1872 (PRT, 1693) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
